D1 Optics L6 Create Reference

Brillouin Scattering off Sound Waves

scatter light from travelling sound waves and measure a gigahertz frequency shift

Thermal sound waves act as a moving diffraction grating. Light Bragg-scatters off them and is Doppler-shifted by a few gigahertz, splitting into a symmetric doublet around the laser line.

Set the sound speed, scattering angle, and refractive index. The Brillouin doublet moves out from the central Rayleigh line; the shift directly reads the material's speed of sound.
